notes: Codex's headless `codex exec` is single-shot, so only variant 1 (clean exit) is recorded for the codex adapter. The spec asserts: (1) session born in ready, (2) process_exited fires after the working window closes, (3) no transition to working or waiting in the tail between final ready and process_exited (state never re-entered working post-exit). Variants 2 and 3 (SIGKILL idle / mid-turn) require driver-level sigkill that codex's interactive driver does not currently expose.

text: Variant 1: session appears in ready within 1s of agent launch
Variant 1: clean exit — process_exited fires after the session has settled back into ready; no transition to waiting between session birth and exit
